timeline.context: Elon Musk was born on June 28, 1971 in Pretoria, South Africa to Errol Musk and Maye Musk
Musk was severely bullied at school in Pretoria, once thrown down a flight of stairs and hospitalized
At age 10, Musk received a Commodore VIC-20 and taught himself to code using a BASIC manual
At age 12, Musk created a video game called Blastar and sold it to a South African magazine for approximately $500

timeline.what: Emigrated from South Africa to Canada
timeline.context: At age 17, Musk left South Africa for Canada to avoid mandatory military service under apartheid
Attended Queen's University in Kingston, Ontario, then transferred to UPenn where he earned bachelor's degrees in physics and economics

timeline.what: Dropped out of Stanford after 2 days
timeline.context: Enrolled in PhD program in energy physics at Stanford, dropped out after just two days to join the Internet revolution
Musk co-founded Zip2 with his brother Kimbal, working out of a small rented office in Palo Alto

timeline.what: Zip2 sold to Compaq for $307 million
timeline.context: Compaq acquired Zip2; Musk received $22 million from the sale
Musk founded X.com, an online financial services company, with his Zip2 proceeds

timeline.what: X.com merged with Confinity, Musk ousted as CEO
timeline.context: X.com merged with Confinity (PayPal); Musk was ousted as CEO during his honeymoon and replaced by Peter Thiel

timeline.what: PayPal acquired by eBay for $1.5 billion
timeline.context: eBay acquired PayPal; Musk received $176 million as largest shareholder
Musk founded SpaceX after a trip to Russia to buy ICBMs failed; he calculated he could build rockets for 10% of the going rate
Musk became Tesla's largest shareholder with a $6.3 million investment and became chairman of the board
SpaceX's first rocket launch failed due to a corroded fuel line nut
Second launch reached space but the second stage spun out of control
Third launch failed when first and second stages collided during separation; SpaceX had money for only one more attempt
Falcon 1 became the first privately developed liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit
NASA awarded SpaceX a contract for 12 cargo flights to ISS; Musk had to borrow money from friends to pay rent
During financial crisis, Musk took over as CEO when Tesla was nearly bankrupt
SpaceX became first private company to dock a spacecraft with the International Space Station
Musk co-founded OpenAI as a nonprofit to develop friendly AI

timeline.what: Founded Neuralink and The Boring Company
timeline.context: Musk co-founded Neuralink for brain-machine interfaces and founded The Boring Company for tunnel construction

timeline.what: Presented Mars colonization plan at IAC
timeline.context: Musk presented his plan to colonize Mars at the International Astronautical Congress in Guadalajara, Mexico
Engineers warned Musk that branding Tesla features as Autopilot was putting customer lives at risk; several engineers resigned

timeline.what: Falcon Heavy launch with Tesla Roadster
timeline.context: SpaceX successfully launched Falcon Heavy with Musk's personal Tesla Roadster as test payload

timeline.what: SEC filed fraud lawsuit over Tesla tweet
timeline.context: Musk tweeted about taking Tesla private at $420; SEC sued for securities fraud
Musk slept on factory floor for months working 120-hour weeks; Tesla came within weeks of bankruptcy
Musk completed $44 billion acquisition of Twitter and laid off approximately 80% of workforce
Musk told advertisers 'Go fuck yourself'; Twitter lost an estimated 79% of equity value
Neuralink implanted its first brain chip in a human patient who could control a computer cursor with thoughts
Musk became largest individual political donor in U.S. history, giving $288 million to Republican candidates through America PAC
Musk was designated special government employee leading DOGE in Trump administration; applied corporate approach to government
Musk stepped down from DOGE role stating the mission would strengthen over time

evidence_cards.summary: Musk described his childhood as severely bullied, being the youngest and smallest kid in class
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I was the youngest and smallest kid in class. I was a prime target.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From Ashlee Vance and Walter Isaacson biographies; quote attributed to Musk himself

evidence_cards.summary: Musk publicly called his father Errol 'a terrible human being'
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: My father is a terrible human being. It was not a happy childhood.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From biographies; Musk has made these statements publicly

evidence_cards.summary: At age 12, Musk created and sold a video game
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: At 12, he created a video game called Blastar and sold it to a South African magazine for approximately $500.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk left South Africa to avoid mandatory military service under apartheid
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I didn't want to be conscripted into the army to fight for apartheid.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From Ashlee Vance biography citing Musk's own statements

evidence_cards.summary: Musk explained why he dropped out of Stanford after 2 days
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I could either watch the Internet revolution happen or I could be part of it. I felt a much stronger pull toward doing something meaningful in the world.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk co-founded Zip2 and coded the website every night, often sleeping at the office
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Musk coded the website every night, often sleeping at the office.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk commented on being ousted as CEO during his honeymoon
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: That's the problem with vacations.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From The PayPal Wars and other sources

evidence_cards.summary: Musk described the low probability of SpaceX succeeding
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I thought the chance of success was less than 10%. I just accepted that I would probably lose everything.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From Ashlee Vance biography and multiple interviews

evidence_cards.summary: SpaceX fourth Falcon 1 launch succeeded, first privately developed liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The fourth launch, on September 28, 2008, succeeded — making Falcon 1 the first privately developed liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk described the stress period as one of the most stressful in his life
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I had to borrow money from friends just to pay rent.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk explained why he took over as Tesla CEO during the financial crisis
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I could either let Tesla die or put all my remaining money into it. That wasn't really a choice.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From Tesla S-1 filing and interviews

evidence_cards.summary: Musk said he wanted to suffer with the workers during Model 3 production crisis
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If the workers are suffering, I want to suffer with them. My suffering should be greater than theirs.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From interviews and Walter Isaacson biography

evidence_cards.summary: Musk called Model 3 production 'the most painful year of my career'
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: It was the most painful year of my career — production hell.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk stated 'humans are underrated' after removing automated systems
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Excessive automation at Tesla was a mistake. Humans are underrated.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From interviews and Walter Isaacson biography

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's tweet that triggered SEC fraud lawsuit
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Am considering taking Tesla private at $420. Funding secured.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's five-step engineering process called 'the algorithm'
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The most common error is to optimize something that shouldn't exist.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From Walter Isaacson biography describing Musk's management style

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's tweet upon completing Twitter acquisition
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: the bird is freed

evidence_cards.summary: Musk laid off approximately 80% of Twitter's workforce
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In the days following the acquisition, Musk laid off approximately 80% of Twitter's workforce.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's confrontation with advertisers at DealBook Summit
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If someone's going to try to blackmail me with advertising, blackmail me with money? Go fuck yourself.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: Recorded at DealBook Summit, video available

evidence_cards.summary: Falcon Heavy launch with Tesla Roadster as test payload
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: On February 6, 2018, SpaceX successfully launched the Falcon Heavy — the most powerful operational rocket in the world at the time.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From SpaceX webcast and multiple reports

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's comment about the Tesla Roadster drifting in space
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I love the thought of a car drifting apparently endlessly through space and perhaps being discovered by an alien race millions of years in the future.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's vision of humanity becoming multi-planetary
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: History is going to bifurcate along two directions. One path is we stay on Earth forever, and then there will be some eventual extinction event. The alternative is to become a space-faring civilization and a multi-planetary species.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk said first Mars journeys will be very dangerous with high fatality risk
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The first journeys to Mars will be very dangerous. The risk of fatality is quite high. There's no way around it.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's desire to die on Mars but not on impact
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I would like to die on Mars. Just not on impact.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk explained first principles thinking
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I tend to approach things from a physics framework. Physics teaches you to reason from first principles rather than by analogy.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's warning about AI as 'summoning the demon'
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: With artificial intelligence we are summoning the demon. You know all those stories where there's the guy with the pentagram and the holy water, and he's like, yeah, he's sure he can control the demon? Doesn't work out.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk co-founded OpenAI as a nonprofit
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: He co-founded OpenAI in 2015 as a nonprofit to develop friendly AI.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk founded xAI as AI competitor
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In 2023, he founded xAI as a competitor.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk on duty to maintain the light of consciousness
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I think we have a duty to maintain the light of consciousness, to make sure it continues into the future.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk on maximizing probability of future being better
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: What I'm trying to do is to maximize the probability of the future being better.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk on changing the world requiring 100-hour work weeks
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Nobody ever changed the world on 40 hours a week.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk described his work schedule of 80-100 hour weeks
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Work like hell. I mean you just have to put in 80 to 100 hour weeks every week.

evidence_cards.summary: Autopilot naming controversy led to engineer resignations
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Several engineers reportedly resigned in 2017 over the naming controversy.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk described entrepreneurship as 'eating glass'
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: Starting a company is like staring into the abyss and eating glass.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk said he'd rather commit seppuku than fail
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I'd rather commit seppuku than fail.

evidence_cards.summary: Neuralink first human brain chip implant
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In January 2024, Neuralink implanted its first brain chip in a human patient.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's rationale for Neuralink as AI mitigation
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If you can't beat them, join them. The merge with AI is the best-case scenario.

evidence_cards.summary: The Boring Company sold flamethrowers to raise funds
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In early 2018 he sold 20,000 branded flamethrowers at $500 each, raising $10 million.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's honest reflection on personal happiness
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: There are many nights I sleep alone.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's first child Nevada died at 10 weeks from SIDS
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: His first child, Nevada, died at 10 weeks from sudden infant death syndrome in 2002.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk returned to work almost immediately after his son's death
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I'm not sure what sitting around being sad is going to do.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From Ashlee Vance biography, reported via colleague

evidence_cards.summary: Isaacson described Musk's 'demon mode' management pattern
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: When Elon is in demon mode, he will fire people, destroy entire teams' work, demand impossible deadlines — and then somehow the product ships.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk was designated special government employee leading DOGE
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In January 2025, Musk was designated a special government employee leading the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in Trump's second administration.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From TIME and various news reports

evidence_cards.summary: Musk stated DOGE mission would strengthen over time
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The DOGE mission will only strengthen over time as it becomes a way of life throughout the government.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk's political shift statement
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I used to be a moderate Democrat. I didn't leave the Democrats. The Democrats left me.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk became largest individual political donor in US history
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In 2024 he became the largest individual political donor in U.S. history, giving $288 million to Republican candidates through his America PAC.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk sent Starlink terminals to Ukraine after Russian invasion
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In February 2022, days after Russia invaded Ukraine, Musk sent Starlink satellite terminals to Ukraine after a direct request from a Ukrainian government official on Twitter.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From Walter Isaacson biography and news reports

evidence_cards.summary: Musk explained why he restricted Starlink near Crimea
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: If I had agreed to their request, SpaceX would be explicitly complicit in a major act of war.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk published Hyperloop white paper
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: In 2013, Musk published a 57-page white paper proposing the Hyperloop.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk said he published Hyperloop to show it was possible
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I just wanted to show it was possible.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk cited Hitchhiker's Guide as formative influence
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: I was around 12 or 15 when I had an existential crisis. I was reading various books on philosophy and religion and I was trying to figure out the meaning of life. Then I read Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy and it pointed out that the real challenge is figuring out what questions to ask.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk personally reviewed code commits to determine productivity
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: He personally reviewed code commits to determine who was productive.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk gave remaining Twitter employees ultimatum
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: He sent an email giving remaining employees an ultimatum: commit to extremely hardcore work or leave with severance. Approximately 1,200 additional employees resigned after the email.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk fired engineers on the spot for disagreeing
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: The book Power Play by Tim Higgins contains accounts of Musk firing engineers on the spot for disagreeing with him, then sometimes rehiring them days later.

evidence_cards.summary: Wall Street Journal described Twitter acquisition as worst deal since 2008 crisis
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: By 2024, the Wall Street Journal described the Twitter acquisition as the worst deal in merger finance since the 2008 financial crisis, with the company having lost an estimated 79% of its equity value.

evidence_cards.summary: Musk moved into Fremont factory and slept on production floor for months
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: During the Model 3 production ramp in mid-2018, Musk moved into the Fremont factory, sleeping on the production floor for months.
evidence_cards.reliability_note: From interviews and Walter Isaacson biography

evidence_cards.summary: Federal judge ruled Musk was de facto leader of DOGE
evidence_cards.verbatim_quote: A federal judge ruled that Musk had acted as the de facto leader of DOGE.
